title: Canada Advances Auto Strategy with $97M for EV Charging and Clean Fuel Projects
content: Following the launch of its new Automotive Strategy on February 5, 2026, the Government of Canada has announced over $97 million in funding for 155 clean transportation projects. A major portion, $84.4 million, is allocated to installing more than 8,000 electric vehicle (EV) chargers across the country through the Zero Emission Vehicle Infrastructure Program, adding to the 30,000 already installed. The strategy also includes a $1.5 billion investment via the Canada Infrastructure Bank for charging and hydrogen refuelling infrastructure. Additionally, $5.7 million will support the Green Freight Program to help fleets adopt low-carbon alternative fuel vehicles and repower existing ones. A further $7.2 million funds 30 education projects, 11 Indigenous-led, to boost awareness of EVs and clean fuels. These initiatives aim to secure Canada's auto industry, promote EV adoption, and create jobs in the supply chain.
author: Analysts Account
